title: vigna owahuensis
intro: Vigna owahuensis, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered yellow-flowering legume native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and soothing respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Vigna owahuensis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although its availability and use are heavily regulated due to its endangered status.
